title: oxalidaceae
intro: The rank of Oxalidaceae refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the wood sorrels or oxalis families. This family comprises about 800 species of plants, many of which are native to the Americas, but can also be found in Africa and Asia. Oxalidaceae plants are typically annual or perennial herbs with leaves that often have a distinctive lobed or divided shape. The family is characterized by its unique flowers, which are often pink, white, or purple in color and have five petals.
